title: Mountains of Fire
editorialSummary: Volcanoes grouped by structural type. Shield volcanoes (broad, low-slope, basaltic): Mauna Loa, Mauna Kea, Olympus Mons, Kilauea. Stratovolcanoes / composite (steep, explosive): Fuji, Vesuvius, Krakatoa, St. Helens. Cinder cone (small, steep, pyroclastic): Paricutin, Cerro Negro, Sunset Crater, Pu'u 'O'o. Lava dome (viscous lava plug): Lassen Peak, Soufriere Hills, Novarupta, Mount Pelee. No false trails — each volcano is a textbook example of its type.
